title: Concentration and extraction of wastewater samples - 35mL
author: Catherine Pratt

steps_raw.instruction: Using a serological pipette, aliquot 35mL of each wastewater sample into the labelled 50mL falcon tube
Invert wastewater samples 5 times to mix. Let sit for 45 second at Room temperature
Pre-heat a 1.5mL tube heat block to 56 °C
Add 100μL of Nanotrap Enhancement Reagent 1 (ER1) to each sample
Close the lids securely and invert 3-5 times to mix
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Invert 3 times to mix, every 5 minutes, during the incubation
Place the tube on a 50mL magnetic rack for 00:10:00 or until beads have separated from the solution
Using a serological pipette, discard the supernatant carefully, without disturbing the pellet
Add 1mL of Nanotrap Buffer 2 OR nuclease-free water to each pellet Wet the beads as you add the buffer/water. Add to all samples before moving onto mixing to avoid over-drying of beads.

Pipette to mix, or close the lids securely and swirl the tubes, to resuspend the beads. If beads remain stuck to the tube wall, use a micropipette to aspirate liquid from the bottom of the tube and then gently dispense the liquid against the tube wall to wash the beads off the wall.
Using a 1mL pipette, transfer the bead suspension into the labelled 1.5mL tube. Be sure to transfer the whole volume, so no liquid remains in the 50mL tube.
Place the tubes on a 1.5mL magnetic rack for 00:02:00 or until the beads have separated from the solution
Using a 1mL pipette, discard the supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet.
Use a smaller pipette to remove the last of the supernatant from the tube, without disturbing the pellet.

Remove the tubes from the magnet and add 500μL of MagMAX Lysis Buffer (prepared with the internal control, if used) from the MagMAX Wastewater Ultra Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit and pipette up and down until fully resuspended
Close the lids securely and verify that each lid is closed. Incubate the samples on the heat block at 56 °C for 00:10:00
Centrifuge the tubes for 3 seconds
Place the tubes on a 1.5mL magnetic rack for 00:02:00 or until beads have separated from the solution

Proceed to Extraction immediately, where possible. If not possible, transfer 400uL of the Ceres-concentrated sample into the appropriate plastic consumable for the nucleic acid extraction step: - 1.5mL tube for manual extraction - 96 Deep Well plate for automated extraction on a Kingfisher instrument. Discard the 1.5mL tube containing the Nanotrap particle pellet. SAFE STOPPING POINT After concentration, samples mixed with lysis buffer may be stored at 4 °C for up to 24:00:00 prior to extraction. Extraction on the same day is strongly recommended to maximize nucleic acid recovery.

Prepare 1.5mL of 80% Ethanol in nuclease-free water for each sample and control. For example: If extracting 11 samples + 1 extraction control = 12 x 1.5mL = 18mL of 80% Ethanol required 18mL x 0.8 = 14.4mL of 100% Ethanol 18mL x 0.2 = 3.6mL of nuclease-free water

Prepare the Binding Bead Mix for the number of samples and controls plus 10%. Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by slowly inverting 5-10 times. Do not pipette to mix. For example: If extracting 11 samples + 1 control = 12 plus 10% overage = prepare Binding Bead Mix for 14 samples Vortex the Binding Beads well before use. Pipette the reagents very slowly as they are viscous. Do not pipette to mix or to rinse the tip after addition. Reagent Volume per sample (uL) 1 MagMAX Binding Beads 20 2 MagMAX Binding Buffer 500 3 Total 520
Add 400μL of the concentrated wastewater/lysis supernatant from the Ceres concentration step to row A, using a separate well for each sample.

Add the following reagents to each required well of a row in the 96 Deep Well plate. Each sample requires one well in each row, for example 10 samples would take 10 wells in the row. Seal the plate after addition with parafilm or an adhesive plate seal to reduce evaporation. Row Reagents 1 A - 2 B 12 - Tip Comb 3 C - 4 D 1mL MagMAX Wash Buffer 5 E 1mL freshly prepared 80% Ethanol 6 F - 7 G - 8 H -

Before beginning: i. Check that the KingFisher Duo has the 12-pin magnet and 12 position heat block installed ii. Check that the KingFisher Duo has the 'MagMAX_Wastewater_DUO96.bdz' protocol script installed

Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by inverting 5-10 times, then add 520μL of the Binding Bead Mix to each sample including the extraction control. Do not pipette to mix with the sample. The KingFisher Duo will perform the mixing. Do not pipette to mix the Binding Bead Mix, only invert. Use a fresh tip per sample when adding to the plate as the mix is extremely viscous and tip re-use may lead to varied volume addition. Invert to mix if the beads begin to settle during addition.
Add 40μL of Proteinase K to each sample including the extraction control. Do not pipette to mix with the sample. The KingFisher Duo will perform the mixing.
Run the 'MagMAX_Wastewater_DUO96' script on the KingFisher Duo and load the plate and elution strip when instructed
Add 100μL of MagMAX Elution Buffer to each tube in the Elution Strip. Close the strip tubes to reduce evaporation. Remember to remove the lids before loading into the KingFisher Duo.
At the end of the run, immediately remove the plate. Seal and centrifuge the plate if possible.

Aliquot extracted nucleic acid into 2 x 50μL aliquots: One aliquot for PCR analysis, which can be used immediately or stored at 4 °C Overnight One aliquot for Sequencing, which should be stored at -20 °C Although the nucleic acid is eluted into 100uL, there may not be sufficient volume to prepare 2x50uL aliquots due to evaporation during the elution step. In that case, aliquot 1x50uL for PCR, and store the remaining volume for sequencing.

Extraction can be performed either manually or using an automated KingFisher instrument. Here we include the protocols for: Manual extraction KingFisher Duo Prime (up to 12 samples per run) KingFisher Flex (up to 96 samples per run)

Before beginning: i. Check that the KingFisher Flex has the 96-pin magnet and 96 Deep Well heat block installed ii. Check that the KingFisher Flex has the 'MagMAX_Wastewater_Flex96_V2.bdz' script installed

Prepare 1.5mL of 80% Ethanol in nuclease-free water for each sample and control. For example: If extracting 95 samples + 1 extraction control = 96 x 1.5mL = 144mL of 80% Ethanol required 144mL x 0.8 = 115.2mL of 100% Ethanol 144mL x 0.2 = 28.8mL nuclease-free Water

Prepare the Binding Bead Mix for the number of samples and controls plus 10%. Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by slowly inverting 5-10 times. Do not pipette to mix. For example: If extracting 95 samples + 1 control = 96 plus 10% overage = prepare Binding Bead Mix for 106 samples Vortex the Binding Beads well before use. Pipette the reagents very slowly as they are viscous. Do not pipette to mix or to rinse the tip after addition. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample 1 MagMAX Binding Buffer 500 2 MagMAX Binding Beads 20 3 Total 520

Label three KingFisher 96 Deep Well plates with the names in the Plate column in the table below. Add the volume of each reagent listed to the named plate, fill sufficient wells for the number of samples and controls that your are extracting. For example: If you are extracting 48 samples and 2 controls, fill 50 wells of the plate. Make sure all plates follow the same layout. Seal the plates with parafilm or an adhesive seal to reduce evaporation. Plate Reagents 1 Wash Plate 1 1mL MagMAX Wash Buffer 2 Wash Plate 2 1mL freshly prepared 80% Ethanol 3 Elution Plate 100μL MagMAX Elution Buffer Reagents from the MagMAX™ Wastewater Ultra Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A52606
Add 400μL of the supernatant from Ceres enrichment to the Sample Plate, using a separate well per sample. Follow the same plate layout as the previous reagent plates.

Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by inverting 5-10 times, then add 520μL of the Binding Bead Mix to each sample including the extraction control. Do not pipette to mix with the sample. The KingFisher Flex will perform the mixing. Do not pipette to mix the Binding Bead Mix, only invert. Use a fresh tip per sample when adding to the plate as the mix is extremely viscous and tip re-use may lead to varied volume addition. Invert to mix if the beads begin to settle during addition.
Add 40μL of Proteinase K to each sample including the extraction control. o not pipette to mix with the sample. The KingFisher Duo will perform the mixing.

Run the 'MagMAX_Wastewater_Flex96_V2.bdz' script on the KingFisher Flex. Load the four prepared plates and a fifth 96 Deep Well plate containing the tip comb into the KingFisher Flex instrument when instructed.
At the end of the run, centrifuge the plate if possible

Aliquot extracted nucleic acid into 2 x 50μL aliquots: One aliquot for PCR analysis, which can be used immediately or stored at 4 °C Overnight One aliquot for Sequencing, which should be stored at -20 °C Although the nucleic acid is eluted into 100uL, there may not be sufficient volume to prepare 2x50uL aliquots due to evaporation during the elution step. In that case, aliquot 1x50uL for PCR, and store the remaining volume for sequencing.
Pre-heat a 1.5mL heat block to 65 °C

Prepare 1.5mL of 80% Ethanol in nuclease-free water for each sample and control. For example: If extracting 15 samples + 1 extraction control = 16 x 1.5mL = 24mL of 80% Ethanol required 24mL x 0.8 = 19.2mL of 100% Ethanol 24mL x 0.2 = 4.8mL nuclease-free water

Prepare the Binding Bead Mix for the number of samples and controls plus 10%. Prepare the Binding Bead Mix for the number of samples and controls plus 10%. Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by slowly inverting 5-10 times. Do not pipette to mix. For example: If extracting 95 samples + 1 control = 96 plus 10% overage = prepare Binding Bead Mix for 106 samples Vortex the Binding Beads well before use. Pipette the reagents very slowly as they are viscous. Do not pipette to mix or to rinse the tip after addition. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample 1 MagMAX Binding Buffer 500 2 MagMAX Binding Beads 20 3 Total 520
Label 2 x 1.5mL tubes for each sample

Mix the Binding Bead Mix well by inverting 5-10 times, then add 520μL of the Binding Bead Mix to each sample including the extraction control. Do not pipette to mix with the sample. Do not pipette to mix the Binding Bead Mix, only invert. Use a fresh tip per sample when adding to the tubes as the mix is extremely viscous and tip re-use may lead to varied volume addition. Invert to mix if the beads begin to settle during addition.
Add 40μL of Proteinase K to each sample tube including the extraction control
Close the lids securely, then vortex to mix
Incubate on the heat block set at 65 °C for 00:05:00
During this incubation, change the temperature on the heat block to 75 °C to prepare for a later step
Spin down the tubes in a micro-centrifuge then place the tubes on a 1.5mL magnetic rack for at least 00:05:00 or until the beads have separated from the solution
Using a 1mL pipette set to 1000uL, discard all of the supernatant, being careful not to touch the beads with the pipette tip.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand and add 1mL of MagMAX Wash Buffer to each tube.
Pipette or vortex to resuspend the beads
Place the tubes back on the 1.5mL magnetic rack for 00:03:00 or until the beads have separated from the solution
Using a 1mL pipette set to 1000uL, discard all of the supernatant, being careful not to touch the beads with the pipette tip.
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand and add 1mL of freshly-prepared 80% ethanol
Place the tubes on a 1.5mL magnetic rack for 00:03:00 or until the beads have separated from the solution
Using a 1mL pipette set to 1000uL, discard all of the supernatant, being careful not to touch the beads with the pipette tip.
Briefly centrifuge the tube and return the tube to the magnetic rack
Remove residual ethanol with a p20 pipette
With the lids open, air-dry the beads for 00:02:00 on the magnetic stand Do not dry for more than 2 minutes, as over-drying can impair DNA elution from the beads
Add 100μL of MagMAX Elution Solution to each tube Note: add buffer to all samples before moving on to avoid over-drying of beads. Wash the Elution solution over the beads as it is added to the tube.
Resuspend the beads by pipetting
Close the lids securely and incubate on a heat block at 75 °C for 00:05:00
Briefly centrifuge the tubes and place on a magnetic stand for 00:03:00 or until the beads have separated from the solution

Aliquot the supernatant into 2 x 50μL aliquots. One for PCR analysis, which can be used immediately or stored at 4 °C Overnight One for Sequencing, which should be stored at -20 °C Although the nucleic acid is eluted into 100uL, there may not be sufficient volume to prepare 2x50uL aliquots due to evaporation during the elution step. In that case, aliquot 1x50uL for PCR, and store the remaining volume for sequencing.
Label 1 x 50mL tube and 1 x 1.5mL tube for each sample
Use a 1mL pipette to remove any remaining liquid from each of the 50mL tubes
Follow the sub-step below to spike the internal control into the lysis buffer. If you are not using internal control, skip this step.
Prepare the mastermix below for the number of samples and controls plus 10%: Reagent Volume (uL) 1 MagMAX Wastewater Lysis Buffer 500 2 Internal Control (10,000 copies/uL) 2.5
Add 525μL of Nanotrap Microbiome A particles to each sample
Close the lids securely and invert 5 times to mix
Add 400μL of nuclease-free water to an empty well in row A to act as the extraction control.
Transfer the prepared plate from the clean area to the biosafety cabinet.
Label one KingFisher 96 Deep Well plate with "Sample Plate".
Add 200uL of Lysis Buffer and 200uL of nuclease-free water to one well of the Sample Plate to act as the extraction control
Add 400μL of the supernatant from Ceres enrichment to a labelled 1.5mL tube for each sample
Add 200uL of Lysis Buffer and 200uL of nuclease-free water to one 1.5mL tube to act as the extraction control
Pipette or vortex to resuspend the beads
Remove the tubes from the magnetic stand
